pr.title: Fix various literals and frame sizes
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Bulk matching cleanup from two scripts: one corrected literals, and one adjusted stack frame sizes by adding, removing, or changing padding. The author says the scripts were run over all nonmatching functions and only kept results that improved total match score, with some false positives. The diff spans 84 source files across fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, item, library, menu, trophy, video, and sysdolphin code. Evidence shows many PAD_STACK or explicit u8 padding changes plus representative literal/control-flow edits. Bot report shows +212 matched-code bytes, +16 matched-data bytes, 2 new matches, 192 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched .sdata2 regression in gmresult. Reviewer approved, praising literal changes while warning that some stack-frame changes may need to be reverted for final matches.

what_changed: Author states two scripts were used: one to find and fix incorrect literals, and one to fix frame sizes by adding or removing padding.
Author states scripts were run on all nonmatching functions and only results that improved total match score were kept, with some false positives.
Changed-file metadata lists 84 touched files, 212 added lines, and 45 deleted lines.
Diff excerpt shows many PAD_STACK additions, removals, or size changes, plus some explicit padding arrays such as u8 _padA[16], u8 _padA[24], and u8 _padA[8].

Diff excerpt shows representative literal changes, including camera pause-eye offsets/distances, ftColl facing_dir comparison from 1.0F to -1.0f, gmresult float argument from 0.09F to 0.11f, and gmstaffroll PAD_STACK(0x60) to PAD_STACK(88).

Bot report recorded +0.01% matched code, +212 matched-code bytes, +16 matched-data bytes, 2 new matches, 192 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched-item regression in main/melee/gm/gmresult .sdata2.
Reviewer ribbanya approved, saying the literal changes are nice and stack frame changes are acceptable, while cautioning that final matches may need to change some stack-frame edits back.

smart_moves.text: Kept the scripted bulk edit bounded by score improvement rather than applying every script suggestion blindly.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: kept only results that improved total match score

smart_moves.text: Separated two mechanical edit families: literal corrections and frame-size padding adjustments.
Accepted that false positives existed, rather than presenting the script output as infallible.
Used automated score reporting to expose both wins and regression risk.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Scripted literal and stack-frame searches can find many small matching improvements, but their output should be filtered by objective score evidence and reviewed for false positives.
Stack-frame padding changes can improve current score without necessarily being stable final-source shape; treat them as narrow matching tactics that may need later reversal.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: many PAD_STACK and u8 padding changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Literal corrections may affect both code and data match reports; watch for data-section regressions even when total text score improves.

naming_conventions.convention: Explicit padding arrays introduced by this PR use local generated-style names such as _padA.
No evidence in this slice supports a broader semantic rename convention; changed symbols largely retain existing address-style names such as fn_80184138 and ftCo_800A2718.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add, remove, resize, or move PAD_STACK calls to influence stack frame size for nonmatching functions.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: fixes frame sizes by adding or removing padding
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Reviewer warned some stack-frame changes may need to be changed back for final matches.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit local u8 padding arrays when PAD_STACK placement is not the selected shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This is a matching tactic, not evidence of preferred source style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Correct float/int literal values when script and match score indicate the existing literal is wrong.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script finds and fixes incorrect literals
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Bot report recorded one .sdata2 regression, so literal edits should be checked against data reports as well as text score.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reshape small boolean/control-flow expressions when the generated expression does not match as well.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800A2718 return false/true nested inlineL0/inlineL1 rewrite
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_800A2718 score improvement
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Only one representative control-flow rewrite is visible in the loaded diff excerpt; do not generalize beyond this PR evidence.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.body: The literal changes are nice. I don't mind the stack frame changes, but I think there will be at least some instances where the final match will need to change it back to what it was before.
review_feedback.actionable_takeaway: Literal fixes were positively received; stack-frame padding edits are acceptable as interim matching improvements but should not be treated as guaranteed final source shape.

searchable_terms: Fix various literals and frame sizes
follow_up_queries: ribbanya stack frame changes final match change back
scripted literal fixes match score false positives

notes: PR identity, author, title, URL, merge timestamp, changed-file list, PR body, bot report, and approval review are loaded.
The raw diff is truncated in the prompt, so detailed hunk-level classification is strongest for the visible excerpt and weaker for later changed files.
Changed-file metadata is complete in loaded changed_files.jsonl and includes 84 files, including vi0401.c, vi0502.c, and texpdag.c.
No inline review comments are present; review feedback is limited to one approving review body.
The author claims score filtering, and the bot report provides aggregate score evidence, but this slice does not include per-edit objdiff/build logs.

Because the PR body explicitly mentions false positives and the reviewer cautions about stack-frame edits, padding changes should be treated as matching tactics rather than accepted source-style standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Scripted literal/frame-size cleanup needs score filtering and false-positive review
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2467 supports a reusable lesson that bulk scripts can identify many literal and frame-size matching improvements, but results should be filtered by match-score evidence and reviewed for false positives.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Stack padding wins may be temporary
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2467 supports treating PAD_STACK or explicit padding-array changes as narrow matching tactics: the reviewer approved but warned that some stack-frame changes may need to be changed back for final matches.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Literal fixes were well received; frame-size edits accepted with caution
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer ribbanya said the literal changes were nice, did not object to stack-frame changes, but expected some final matches may need to revert those stack-frame edits.

source_update_candidates.title: Document bulk-scripted matching cleanup safeguards
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-linking guidance that bulk scripts for literals or stack-frame padding should keep only edits backed by narrow score/objdiff evidence, explicitly note false-positive handling, and avoid treating PAD_STACK or padding arrays as final source style without later cleanup review.
source_update_candidates.reason: Current loaded standards already cover matching tactics needing evidence, but this PR provides concrete historical evidence for bulk-script false-positive filtering and the temporary nature of stack-frame padding edits.

rejection_notes: Do not promote _padA as a global naming standard; evidence only shows PR-local explicit padding variable names.
Do not promote PAD_STACK additions as preferred final style; reviewer explicitly warned some stack-frame changes may need changing back.
Do not claim every changed file improved individually; the bot report gives aggregate results and selected examples, while the full per-edit validation is not loaded.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the approval text; only literal changes were explicitly praised and stack-frame changes explicitly accepted with caution.
Do not treat the gmresult .sdata2 regression as necessarily unacceptable; the PR was merged, but the slice does not explain the tradeoff beyond the bot report.
